title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 31 - A look at Gates of Valhalla (retail)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fFgFYRumrDU

---

Daniel (@dungeondive) returns to Shadows of Brimstone for a detailed look at Gates of Valhalla, a smaller retail core set featuring four Viking heroes on an eight-mission campaign through the Norse lands and the Targa Plateau library.

The Vikings are all the same class but differentiated by stats, starting gear, and rotating team ability cards drawn fresh each mission. The rage mechanic — generated by taking wounds, spent to boost damage — combined with Battle Fury's heal-on-kill creates an aggressive playstyle distinct from other Shadows of Brimstone characters.

The Viking Camp replaces the frontier town with upgradeable locations (campfire, healer, expedition supplies) that unlock new options as you invest gold and pelts. A hunting mini-game, campfire tales for XP, and meat-cooking mechanics add flavour. Daniel appreciates the smaller map tile set (10 vs the original 19), which creates shorter, tighter sessions.

Enemy variety is the set's weakness — trolls, frost giants, grimians, centurions, tentacles (reused), and Targa librarians feel thin for extended play. Daniel supplements with 11 additional enemy groups from his collection including flesh stalkers, void swarms, Belial, and the Shakari Nomad Crusaders.

Flying Frog sent Daniel the review copy after discovering his deep-dive series. He praises the Viking theme execution and camp upgrades but wants at least two more enemy types and is eagerly awaiting the Norse lands card supplement packs.
